behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific details not mentioned in instruction.md: test_repo_ruff_lint and test_repo_ruff_format check 'areal/experimental/' directory with ruff 0.14.9 (instruction mentions ruff version but not the exact directory path used in subprocess). More critically, tests like test_router_gate_linear_class_exists assert `hasattr(router.gate, 'router_dtype')` — while instruction.md says the subclass must accept `router_dtype` in __init__, it doesn't explicitly state that `router_dtype` must be

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md describes what's broken (DTensor hooks don't fire because gate is bypassed) and requirements (nn.Linear subclass, forward delegates to router_gating_linear, etc.) but doesn't specify exact line changes, line numbers, or the exact code to write.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavioral properties: hook fires during forward, gate has router_dtype attribute, gate is nn.Module, state_dict keys match nn.Linear(bias=False). Alternative implementations (e.g., different class names, different internal structure) would pass as long as they satisfy these behavioral constraints. Tests don't assert on the class name 'RouterGateLinear' specifically.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests actually import the module, instantiate TokenChoiceTopKRouter, run forward passes, register hooks, check hook firing counts, verify output shapes, and check state_dict keys. Only test_syntax_check uses py_compile, and ruff tests run actual linting — none are pure text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_gate_hook_fires, test_gate_hook_with_varied_inputs, test_router_gate_linear_class_exists, test_gate_is_router_gate_linear) all require real behavioral changes. A stub/no-op wouldn't fire hooks or have router_dtype attribute. The base code (plain nn.Linear gate called via direct function) would fail these tests.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four requirements from instruction.md are tested: (1) gate module invocation with hook firing (test_gate_hook_fires), (2) GEMM precision via router_gating_linear delegation (test_gate_is_router_gate_linear checks router_dtype), (3) checkpoint compatibility (test_state_dict_compat), (4) nn.Linear subclass with router_dtype (test_gate_is_router_gate_linear, test_router_gate_linear_class_exists).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-structured with clear section headers (Static checks, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass), descriptive function names, detailed docstrings explaining what each test verifies and why, and comments referencing the check categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves fixing a Python module, not producing structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos detected in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in instruction.md or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md explicitly names 'router_gating_linear()' as the function to delegate to, specifies the exact __init__ signature (in_features, out_features, router_dtype), and states the gate must be an nn.Linear subclass — this significantly narrows the solution space beyond just describing the bug. The instruction essentially prescribes the fix architecture (requirement #4).

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config-edit task.
